description: Keeps OpenTelemetry Collector version pins in sync across TheWeatherCompany repos. A repo opts in by adding to its renovate.json: "extends": ["github>TheWeatherCompany/mwt-renovate-config:otel-collector"]. See README.md for which repos/files this is meant to cover and why.

customManagers.description: mwt-ee-deploy: Helm chart version for the EKS otel-collector deployment (terraform/compute/services/otel-collector/environments/{dev,stg,prod}.tfvars). Public upstream (open-telemetry Helm repo) - no gating needed.
mwt-ee-deploy: otelcol-contrib image tag baked into the Helm values (same tfvars files as above, separate knob). Public upstream (Docker Hub) - no gating needed.

EC2-classic per-app agent config consumed by mwt-deploy's installOpenTelemetry.sh, e.g. mwt-config-api/scripts/otel-api.json, mwt-forecasteditor-api/otel-*.json, mwt-widgets/otel-web.json, etc. Gated: this datasource is TheWeatherCompany/mwt-observability's own GitHub releases, and mwt-observability's update-observability.yml only tags an otel-collector-vX release *after* sync-binaries has mirrored that version's RPM to S3. This prevents proposing a version that installOpenTelemetry.sh would 404 on at deploy time.
mwt-perforce-deploy: same RPM as above, pinned as a Terraform variable default instead of a JSON config (modules/perforce-server/variables.tf). Same gated datasource as the JSON manager above.

packageRules.description: Ordered broadest-first: Renovate merges packageRules in order, later rules override earlier ones on conflicting keys, so more specific tiers below must come after this one to win. Default: treat any file not matched below as prod-tier. Longer soak, and never automerge an OTel Collector bump anywhere - a human always reviews the PR before it ships.
Staging-tier files: let dev soak for about a week first.
Dev-tier files: propose immediately, no soak required.

One-time bootstrap: otel-collector-v0.156.0 was backfilled as a GitHub release on 2026-07-14, for a version that had actually been mirrored to S3 weeks earlier (before the gate-release step existed). Without this, the prod-tier 14-day minimumReleaseAge above would count from the backfilled release's creation time, not the real mirror date, delaying the very first catch-up bump for no reason. Remove this rule once 0.156.0 has rolled out everywhere - future releases go through the normal tiered soak above. Waive the soak for the backfilled 0.156.0 release only.
